skill.name: suggesting-improvements
skill.description: Provides structured guidance for improving AI responses, code suggestions, explanations, and completed work through specific, prioritized recommendations.
skill.summary: Turn quality issues into concrete, prioritized improvements for AI-assisted work.
skill.author: C0ntr0lledCha0s

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many high-risk patterns, but review found they are markdown templates and illustrative code examples inside SKILL.md. No executable script, real shell execution, network request, weak cryptographic implementation, system reconnaissance, or prompt injection attempt was found.

low_findings.title: Static Shell Execution Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The external command detections are markdown code fences and before/after examples, not executable Ruby or shell commands. The skill declares only Read, Grep, and Glob tools.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited ranges are fenced markdown templates or example snippets in an instruction document. No file in the skill defines runnable Ruby, shell, or automation code.

low_findings.title: Static Network Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The network detections are example function names and request-handling placeholders inside illustrative Python snippets. No HTTP client call or external endpoint is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are part of a generic refactoring example using handle_request and validate_request names. I found no URL, HTTP library invocation, or data exfiltration path.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections appear to match ordinary words such as description, API Design, and Solution Design. No cryptographic algorithm is implemented or recommended.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited context contains descriptive headings and metadata, not cryptographic code. No evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, or similar weak algorithm usage was found in the skill behavior.

low_findings.title: Static System Reconnaissance Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance detections are ordinary instructional text and example validation code. No command or instruction gathers host, network, user, or environment details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are within templates or benign validation/documentation examples. I found no semantic intent to profile the system or collect sensitive host information.

content.user_title: Improve AI Responses With Actionable Feedback
content.value_statement: AI-assisted work can miss edge cases, clarity issues, or better implementation paths. This skill turns those gaps into prioritized, concrete improvement suggestions.
actual_capabilities: Identifies specific issues in responses, code, explanations, and solution plans.
Groups recommendations by correctness, completeness, clarity, efficiency, security, and usability.
Prioritizes fixes by impact, urgency, cost, and practical value.
Provides before-and-after examples for proposed improvements.
Suggests verification steps and success criteria for each improvement.
limitations: It does not execute code or verify runtime behavior by itself.
It depends on the quality and completeness of the material being reviewed.
It cannot guarantee that every suggested improvement is the best option for all contexts.
It provides guidance, not automatic implementation.

use_cases.title: Improve a coding answer
use_cases.description: Review an AI-generated coding response and receive specific changes for correctness, tests, error handling, and maintainability.

use_cases.title: Refine technical explanations
use_cases.description: Find unclear or missing parts in an explanation and turn them into a clearer structure with better examples.

use_cases.title: Prioritize product feedback
use_cases.description: Convert broad feedback about an AI output into ranked improvements with implementation steps and trade-offs.

prompt_templates.title: Find basic improvements
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a short response
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this response and suggest three specific improvements. Include the issue, why it matters, and the exact change to make.

prompt_templates.title: Improve code quality
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused review of code or pseudocode
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this code-focused answer for correctness, missing edge cases, security, and maintainability. Prioritize the changes and include verification steps.

prompt_templates.title: Refine an explanation
prompt_templates.scenario: Improving clarity for a target audience
prompt_templates.prompt: Suggest improvements to make this explanation clearer for the stated audience. Include structure changes, missing context, and better examples.

prompt_templates.title: Create an improvement roadmap
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this solution and produce a prioritized improvement roadmap. Separate must-fix issues, important enhancements, optional refinements, trade-offs, and success criteria.

output_examples.input: A generated answer solves the main task but has weak error handling and no tests.
output_examples.output: The skill would identify the missing failure paths, recommend targeted validation, add practical test scenarios, and rank the fixes by impact.

output_examples.input: A technical explanation uses dense terms and assumes too much prior knowledge.
output_examples.output: The skill would suggest a simpler opening, define key terms, reorder the explanation, and add a concrete example for the audience.

output_examples.input: A proposed implementation is correct but more complex than necessary.
output_examples.output: The skill would point out the complexity, propose a simpler alternative, explain trade-offs, and define checks to confirm behavior is unchanged.

best_practices: Provide the original response or code with the user goal and audience.
Ask for prioritized suggestions when time or scope is limited.
Request verification steps so improvements can be checked after implementation.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for vague advice without sharing the output that needs improvement.
Do not treat every suggestion as mandatory when trade-offs matter.
Do not skip verification after applying a recommended change.

faq.question: What does this skill improve?
faq.answer: It improves AI responses, code suggestions, explanations, plans, and completed work by making feedback specific and actionable.

faq.question: Does it make code changes automatically?
faq.answer: No. It recommends changes and verification steps, but implementation requires a separate action.

faq.question: Can it review non-code writing?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for clarity, structure, completeness, examples, and audience fit.

faq.question: How should I use it with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Give the tool the current output, the goal, and the constraints. Ask for ranked improvements and success criteria.

faq.question: Does it handle security feedback?
faq.answer: Yes. It can suggest security hardening ideas, but findings should be validated before release.

faq.question: What makes its feedback useful?
faq.answer: It links each issue to a concrete change, explains the benefit, and includes practical ways to verify the improvement.
